reviews.author: Thị Hài Nguyễn
reviews.text: Located at the southernmost point of Vietnam, immerse yourself in the sea and enjoy the natural seafood. You can easily travel by taxi from Ca Mau airport, and the locals are friendly.

reviews.author: Liễu Trần
reviews.text: Mui Ca Mau National Park is a peaceful, magical corner at Vietnam’s southernmost tip. Surrounded by mangrove forests and winding waterways, it’s a must-visit for nature lovers. I took a boat ride through the lush green, spotted birds, crabs, and even monkeys! The air is fresh, the vibes are calm, and the view from the iconic GPS 0001 marker is unforgettable. It’s not touristy, which makes it even more special. Bring your camera and curiosity—you’ll leave with a heart full of nature and pride in this southern land. A truly grounding and beautiful experience!

reviews.author: Huynh Vinh
reviews.text: There is nothing to complain about the view, which is marvelous and spectacular. The bridge was originally intended to link the park to Hon Khoai Island, but owing to a dispute related to the local law the project had been abandoned for long. The bridge or this small dock is locked for visitors' safety but for the true beauty of nature you can easily climb over the door. One thing I just dont understand why this bridge cannot be kept building, leaving it really untidy and dangerous. This place is a must-see one when you are in Ca Mau.

reviews.author: Huy Nguyen
reviews.text: The National Park of Cape Cà Mau is a hidden gem in southern Vietnam that promises an extraordinary journey into the heart of nature. With a staggering amount of biodiversity and its unique location on the southernmost tip of Vietnam's territory, this national park is an absolute must-visit destination for any nature enthusiast. One of the most remarkable aspects of this park is its rich variety of wildlife. Boasting an impressive roster of inhabitants, including 26 species of mammals, 43 species of reptiles, 9 species of amphibians, 233 species of fish, and 93 species of birds, the National Park of Cape Cà Mau serves as a living testament to the exceptional biodiversity of the region. Every step taken within the park's boundaries offers the chance to encounter rare and fascinating creatures, making it a haven for wildlife enthusiasts and researchers alike. The sheer beauty of the National Park of Cape Cà Mau is awe-inspiring. Towering trees, lush greenery, and meandering waterways create a landscape that feels like a fairytale come to life. The park's pristine environment serves as a sanctuary for both the flora and fauna, providing a harmonious habitat for the diverse range of species that call it home. Exploring its trails and soaking in the breathtaking views allows visitors to reconnect with nature and experience an unparalleled sense of tranquility. Furthermore, the location of this national park adds tremendous allure to its already impressive repertoire. Situated on the southernmost tip of Vietnam's territory, the park provides a gateway to a world that feels untouched and unexplored. The combination of its remote location, untouched beaches, and the convergence of two vast oceans create a unique atmosphere that captivates the imagination and fuels a sense of adventure. Visiting the National Park of Cape Cà Mau is not only an opportunity to connect with nature's wonders but also a chance to contribute to its preservation. The park's dedication to conservation and sustainable tourism ensures that future generations will be able to witness the splendor it holds. By supporting this endeavor, visitors actively participate in the preservation of an ecosystem teeming with life, and the impact of their visit extends far beyond their personal experience. In conclusion, the National Park of Cape Cà Mau is a magnificent treasure hidden in the southernmost region of Vietnam. Its exceptional biodiversity, breathtaking scenery, and remote location create an unparalleled experience for those fortunate enough to explore it. A visit to this national park is not just a sightseeing adventure; it is an opportunity to witness nature at its most awe-inspiring and to play a role in its preservation. With each step taken in this haven of flora and fauna, visitors are sure to embrace the profound beauty and majesty of the natural world.

reviews.author: Google User
reviews.text: It's fun to come to the end of mainland Vietnam, but there's not a lot to see. There was a lot of construction when we were there. A few monuments, some jungle walks, and a REALLY expensive jungle resturant. Still, I'm glad I can say that I've been to the end of Vietnam, however on the map it looks like Hon Sao island is the most southern point of Vietnam.

reviews.author: John Thuấn
reviews.text: Very exciting place, it's worth to visit in our life.

reviews.author: Yian Kian Lee
reviews.text: This is where one of the 5 GPS 0001 is located throughout Vietnam. Worth travelling all the way down south, the very tail end of Vietnam to "check in". Word of caution though, to bring anykind of protection gear or lotions to protect your skin from sunburn. It would have received a full five star rating if the F&B facilities around is better and has better food and more managed, only available at the main entrance, and another located within the park itself. Other than that, full 5 stars for the location alone, nature lowers and bird watchers ..

reviews.author: Tâm Phan Minh
reviews.text: Such a nice place to enjoy the beautiful mangrove forest

reviews.author: Ben Le
reviews.text: Peak view ngl Also btw imma nsikic you

reviews.author: Duong Trong Tuan
reviews.text: construction is not complete yet, messy and trash, this is CA MAU cape, the end point of vietnam map

reviews.author: Duong Trinh
reviews.text: Its quite nice except too far from the city if going by motorcycle

reviews.author: Jeff Miller
reviews.text: Not bad, but very little to see. We got there first thing in the morning (8am) and were the only people there until about 9 which was nice. The boat ride down from Nam Can was the best part but it was a bit on the pricey side. Still a nice landmark to see and it appears as if they are fixing it up a bit.

reviews.author: anh duc Bui
reviews.text: Natural forest with cool wind and special food

reviews.author: ngodinh tra
reviews.text: It's a special destination, especially for Vietnamese people, since it's the South point of the country. The scenes on the way to travel there (water way) is the unique, you will not see the life style like that in other places. The life on the watery world shows how hard people survive in the mangroves, also how rich the nature in Mekong-Delta was. It quite difficult and requires quite much time to been there, but I still highly recommend.

reviews.author: Victor - Pham Thang
reviews.text: This is beautiful national park. I went by boat, saw river, and monkey, bird, when low tide i saw beautiful crabs... And very good view... U can travel to Ca Mau national park by bus, motorcycle from Ho Chi Minh to Ca Mau city, and then u can go by boat if u want. Or you can go by motorcycle to Nam Can district, then go by boat through the national park, along beautiful river....

reviews.author: Malcolm Webster
reviews.text: If they have a main entrance we certainly could not find it. Or any decent information to follow. All we got to was a delapatated viewing tower, which was very hard find the entry to. That was after we went down a very narrow road which ended up at a no through unmanned gate. Which was virtually impossible to turn around. Hugely disappointed with anything to do with this place. Even being with Vietnamese guides did not make a difference. Before even considering this place, look them up & call them for information.

reviews.author: Hoàng Lê Giang
reviews.text: It could have been a beautiful spot but they made ugly concrete junk everywhere. Should have left it raw , just a wooden pathway instead of building hideous structure and statues.

reviews.author: Hung-Man VU
reviews.text: like this place, the end of vietnam

reviews.author: Nihal Ranjan
reviews.text: This is the end of Indochina peninsula and is definitely worth a visit. Sunset and sunrise are highlights

reviews.author: Anh Tran
reviews.text: Gorgeous sightseeing and historical, thanks for sharing and keeping up the good work 😀

reviews.author: DAC NGUYEN TAT
reviews.text: Great to be at the farthest South end of the country, crabs are good but should ask prices before ordering, boat trips are quite long, 2 hours each

reviews.author: Vu Quang
reviews.text: Under construction of 'Hanoi flag tower in Ca Mau'. Street is under widening. The southeast cape is renovating. It may be completed at around end of 2018

reviews.author: Pham Phuong Thao
reviews.text: ...the ending point of The S- shaped VN

reviews.author: Bac Hai Nguyen
reviews.text: The most famous landscape in Ca Mau and also in eastern-southern provinces of Vietnam

reviews.author: An Nguyen (Cuckoo)
reviews.text: Hard to come here by motorbike Take about 3-4 hour. It is the end of South Viet Nam.

reviews.author: Hiet Nguyen Dinh
reviews.text: Most natural park. The road to cape Camau is very nice.
